name: Sift Science Fraud Detection API
what_it_does: ML-powered fraud detection and trust/safety platform with REST API for real-time transaction risk scoring, account takeover prevention, payment fraud, and content abuse detection.
use_cases: Real-time fraud scoring on payment transactions before authorization
Account takeover detection via behavioral signals and device fingerprinting
Automated fraud review queues with risk-based routing
Content abuse detection for UGC platforms (spam, scam, fake reviews)
Chargeback dispute automation using transaction evidence data
not_for: Simple rule-based fraud prevention without ML (use basic AVS/CVV checks instead)
Organizations with fewer than 10k monthly transactions where setup cost is disproportionate
Pure AML/KYC compliance — Sift focuses on behavioral fraud, not identity verification
best_when: An agent handles payment processing or account actions at scale and needs real-time ML-based risk signals to make block/allow/review decisions.
avoid_when: You need KYC/identity verification — use Persona, Onfido, or Jumio instead.

auth.notes: API key authentication. Separate keys for production and sandbox. Keys included in request body (not header — unusual). REST API key required for all event and decision calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; sandbox available for testing
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing based on monthly API calls; typically $1k-$10k+/month
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing model — contact sales for rates. Pricing based on API call volume and features. Sandbox environment available for development and testing.

performance.rate_limits: High throughput designed for real-time transaction processing

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with SDK support for 7 languages. Event types are clearly defined. Score responses include signals and reasons. Webhook delivery for async decisions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Events can be resent — Sift deduplicates by event ID. Decisions are deterministic given same event data.
known_agent_gotchas: API key goes in request body (api_key field), not Authorization header — unusual pattern
Score APIs and Decision APIs are separate endpoints with different response schemas
Real-time scoring requires sending events first; scores aren't available without event context
Webhook verification requires signature validation — implement HMAC-SHA256 verification

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II and PCI-DSS compliant. GDPR with EU data residency option. API key in request body (not header) is a non-standard pattern. HTTPS enforced. Sandbox isolation from production.
